items.text: Truth enlightens.
items.note: Inlustrat is the archaic spelling of illustrat — “truth illuminates / enlightens.”

items.text: Now comes the next big step, which will be huge, as the pipelines converge. Democratization of curation. Democratization of marketing. How do we point toward what matters? That's where it's going — to influencers, to people like you and people like me, who people trust. Those voices will be more resonant as we move forward, because people will trust them more as we move forward.
items.source: Morgan Spurlock, The Tim Ferriss Show #150 (Mar 29, 2016)
items.note: Transcribed from the episode; wording lightly tidied from the spoken delivery.

items.text: If you want to go fast, go alone. If you want to go far, go together.
items.note: Origin uncertain — universally cited as an African proverb, but never traced to a documented source.

items.text: The greatest insult a man can level at another man is indifference.
items.note: Origin unverified — likely drifting from Elie Wiesel's “the opposite of love is indifference.”

items.text: I walk to where the stream runs out, and sit to watch the clouds rise.
items.original: 行到水窮處，坐看雲起時
items.translation: I stroll along the stream up to where it ends. I sit down watching the clouds as they begin to rise.
items.source: Wang Wei 王維, 《終南別業》
items.note: Tang-dynasty Chan (Zen) poem — the celebrated fifth and sixth lines.

items.text: Art is never finished, only abandoned.
items.note: Commonly misattributed to Leonardo da Vinci. Valéry wrote it of a work (his poem) in a 1933 essay on Le Cimetière marin.

items.title: Bruce Lee
items.text: Be water, my friend.
items.source: 18 reflections · Jeet Kune Do, interviews & films
items.note: Curated to genuine Lee sources. A few lines often misattributed to him were left out — "knowing is not enough…" (Goethe), "love is like a friendship caught on fire" (Jeremy Taylor), and "the key to immortality…" (from the 1993 film Dragon).
entries: Absorb what is useful, discard what is not, add what is uniquely your own.
Simplicity is the last step of art.
A teacher is never a giver of truth — he is a guide, a pointer to the truth that each student must find for himself. A good teacher is merely a catalyst.
When an opportunity in a fight presents itself, "I" don't hit, "it" hits all by itself.
Empty your mind. Become formless and shapeless like water. When water is poured into a cup, it becomes the cup. When water is poured into a teapot, it becomes the teapot. Be water, my friend.

To know oneself is to study oneself in action with another person. If you want to understand the truth in martial arts, to see any opponent clearly, you must throw away the notion of styles or schools, prejudices, likes and dislikes, and so forth. Then your mind will cease all conflict and come to rest. In this silence, you will see totally and freshly.
I fear not the man who has practiced 10,000 kicks once, but I fear the man who has practiced one kick 10,000 times.
Don't think, feel! It is like a finger pointing away to the moon. Don't concentrate on the finger or you will miss all that heavenly glory.
A quick temper will make a fool of you soon enough.
I don't believe in different ways of fighting now. Unless human beings have three arms and three legs, there will be only one way of fighting — and that is no way.

If you always put limits on what you can do, physical or anything else, it'll spread over into the rest of your life. There are no limits. There are plateaus, but you must not stay there; you must go beyond them.
Boards don't hit back.
Ever since I was a child I have had this instinctive urge for expansion and growth. To me, the function and duty of a quality human being is the sincere and honest development of one's potential.
Notice that the stiffest tree is most easily cracked, while the bamboo or willow survives by bending with the wind.
Martial art is ultimately an athletic expression of the dynamic human body. More important yet is the person who is expressing his own soul.
I have always been a martial artist by choice, an actor by profession, but above all, am actualizing myself someday to be an artist of life.
A goal is not always meant to be reached; it often serves simply as something to aim at.
The martial arts are ultimately self-knowledge. A punch or a kick is not to knock the hell out of the guy in front, but to knock the hell out of your ego, your fear, or your hang-ups.

items.text: Listen, I'm a politician, which means I'm a cheat and a liar, and when I'm not kissing babies I'm stealing their lollipops. But it also means I keep my options open.
items.source: Jeffrey Pelt, The Hunt for Red October (1990)

items.text: Health is a crown worn by the healthy that only the sick can see.
items.note: Sometimes attributed to al-Shāfiʿī or Ali ibn Abi Talib, but best cited simply as an Arabic proverb.

items.text: These days, better to appear guilty than impotent.
items.source: President Nemerov, The Sum of All Fears (2002)

items.text: Rather go without than settle for shoddy.
items.translation: Better to have less but better than more and worse.
items.note: Quality over quantity; never lower the standard just to fill a gap.

items.text: I have made this longer than usual because I did not have the time to make it shorter.
items.original: Je n'ai fait celle-ci plus longue que parce que je n'ai pas eu le loisir de la faire plus courte.
items.translation: I have made this longer than usual because I did not have the time to make it shorter.
items.source: Blaise Pascal, Lettres provinciales, Letter XVI (1657)
items.note: Frequently misattributed to Mark Twain and others; Pascal is the documented origin.

items.text: A good idea is meaningless without the courage to act. You must speak up.
Life and Death Are Wearing Me Out.
items.translation: Life and Death Are Wearing Me Out.
items.note: Title of Mo Yan's 2006 novel; English translation by Howard Goldblatt (2008).

items.text: Knowing is not enough; we must apply. Willing is not enough; we must do.
items.note: From Wilhelm Meister's Journeyman Years. Frequently misattributed to Bruce Lee, who quoted it.

items.text: Ride a donkey while you look for a horse.
items.translation: Ride a donkey, look for a horse.
items.note: Make do with what you have while you search for something better.

items.title: Ed Catmull
items.text: If there is more truth in the hallways than in meetings, you have a problem.
items.source: Creativity, Inc. · 6 lessons on leading creative work
items.note: President of Pixar and Walt Disney Animation; from his book Creativity, Inc. (2014).
entries: If there is more truth in the hallways than in meetings, you have a problem.
The desire for everything to run smoothly is a false goal.
The cost of preventing errors is often far greater than the cost of fixing them.

Rules can simplify life for managers, but they can be demeaning to the 95 percent who behave well. Don't create rules to rein in the other 5 percent — address abuses of common sense individually. This is more work but ultimately healthier.
The first conclusions we draw from our successes and failures are typically wrong. Measuring the outcome without evaluating the process is deceiving.

An organization, as a whole, is more conservative and resistant to change than the individuals who comprise it. Do not assume that general agreement will lead to change — it takes substantial energy to move a group, even when all are on board.

items.title: If—
items.text: If you can keep your head when all about you are losing theirs and blaming it on you…
items.note: Recited by Tommy (Jason Clarke) to his fellow representatives in the Rhode Island state legislature in the series Brotherhood.
entries:
If you can keep your head when all about you
Are losing theirs and blaming it on you,
If you can trust yourself when all men doubt you,
But make allowance for their doubting too;
If you can wait and not be tired by waiting,
Or being lied about, don't deal in lies,
Or being hated, don't give way to hating,
And yet don't look too good, nor talk too wise:

If you can make one heap of all your winnings
And risk it on one turn of pitch-and-toss,
And lose, and start again at your beginnings
And never breathe a word about your loss;
If you can force your heart and nerve and sinew
To serve your turn long after they are gone,
And so hold on when there is nothing in you
Except the Will which says to them: "Hold on!"

If you can talk with crowds and keep your virtue,
Or walk with Kings — nor lose the common touch,
If neither foes nor loving friends can hurt you,
If all men count with you, but none too much;
If you can fill the unforgiving minute
With sixty seconds' worth of distance run,
Yours is the Earth and everything that's in it,
And — which is more — you'll be a Man, my son!

items.text: Seven emotions, six desires.
items.translation: The seven emotions and six desires.
items.note: The full range of human feeling and wanting. The seven emotions trace to the Book of Rites; the six desires to the Lüshi Chunqiu and Buddhist tradition.

items.text: I choose a lazy person to do a hard job, because a lazy person will find an easy way to do it.
items.note: Commonly misattributed to Bill Gates. The idea traces to efficiency expert Frank Gilbreth Sr. (1920) and the phrasing to Clarence Bleicher (1947).

items.text: Strength doesn't come from what you can do. It comes from overcoming the things you once thought you couldn't.

items.title: Jony Ive
items.text: It's very easy to be different, but very difficult to be better.
items.source: 17 reflections · interviews, design talks & Apple commentary
items.note: Compiled from Ive's interviews and talks; several lines are widely quoted with slightly varying phrasings, and near-duplicate versions were consolidated here.
entries: True simplicity is, well, you just keep on going and going until you get to the point where you go, 'Yeah, well, of course.' Where there's no rational alternative.

Simplicity is not the absence of clutter — that's a consequence of simplicity. Simplicity is somehow essentially describing the purpose and place of an object and product. The absence of clutter is just a clutter-free product. That's not simple.
It's very easy to be different, but very difficult to be better.
There's no learning without trying lots of ideas and failing lots of times.
We shouldn't be afraid to fail — if we're not failing, we're not pushing. Eighty percent of the stuff in the studio is not going to work. If something is not good enough, stop doing it.
There is beauty when something works, and it works intuitively.
If you are truly innovating, you don't have a prototype you can refer to.
What we make stands testament to who we are. People can sense care, and they can sense carelessness.

I believe that when somebody unwrapped that box and took out that cable, they thought, 'Somebody gave a shit about me.' Even though it was a small thing, it really did come genuinely from a place of love and of care.
Apple's goal isn't to make money. Our goal is to design and develop and bring to market good products.

What I love about the creative process — and this may sound naive — is this idea that one day there is no idea and no solution, and the next day there is. I find that incredibly exciting, and conceptually remarkable.
A small change at the beginning of the design process defines an entirely different product at the end.
When something exceeds your ability to understand how it works, it sort of becomes magical.
Designing and developing anything of consequence is incredibly challenging.
Making the solution seem so completely inevitable and obvious, so uncontrived and natural — it's so hard!
It's a very strange thing for a designer to say, but one of the things that really irritates me in products is when I'm aware of designers wagging their tails in my face.
The best ideas start as conversations.

items.text: Life is a collection of moments. The idea is to have as many good ones as you can.
items.note: Written and directed by Victor Levin. Quote Catalog attributes the line to Arielle (Berenice Marlohe); some search listings credit Jane (Olivia Thirlby). The line is confirmed as from the film; the speaking character is single-sourced and not verified against the film itself, so no character is asserted here.

items.text: If you wanna be a good writer, then you can't have a mediocre life.
items.note: Trimmed from the full line: "Look, if you wanna be a good writer then you can't have a mediocre life. I'll give you a baseball metaphor, because Jewish writers love them. Swing from the heels." Speaker (Jane, played by Olivia Thirlby) confirmed by two independent sources.

items.text: I will hold your heart more tenderly than my own.
items.note: From the film's wedding-vow sequence. Confirmed as from 5 to 7 by multiple secondary sources and referenced by writer-director Victor Levin in his Roger Ebert interview; the speaking character is not listed in the quote databases, so none is asserted.

items.text: As little as you want to write when you're happy, that's how much you have to write when you're miserable. Your passions need to go somewhere, and this is the only place left. Your suffering has to be good for something. It's not for me to say if the words were worth the price.
items.note: Speaker (Brian Bloom, played by Anton Yelchin) confirmed by two independent sources (Quote Catalog, quotes.net). Verified transcripts read "your passions need to go somewhere"; some renderings say "have to."
